segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: What kind of race do the children compete in?

translation.text: 孩子们参加的是哪种比赛？

segments.text: Experiments have proved that children can be instructed in swimming at a very early age.

translation.text: 实验已经证明，孩子很小的时候就可以学习游泳。

segments.text: Underwater, even before they can walk.

translation.text: 甚至在学会走路之前，就可以在水下训练。

segments.text: Babies of two months old do not appear to be reluctant to enter the water.

translation.text: 两个月大的婴儿似乎并不抗拒下水。

segments.text: It is not long before they are so accustomed to swimming that they can pick up weights from the floor of the pool.

translation.text: 没过多久，他们就会习惯游泳，甚至能从池底捡起重物。

segments.text: A game that is very popular with these young swimmers is the underwater tricycle race.

translation.text: 这些小游泳者非常喜欢的一项游戏是水下三轮车比赛。

segments.text: Tricycles are lined up on the floor of the pool seven feet underwater.

translation.text: 三轮车在水深七英尺的池底排成一列。

segments.text: The children compete against each other to reach the other end of the pool.

translation.text: 孩子们相互竞赛，看谁先到达游泳池另一端。

segments.text: Many pedal their tricycles, but most of them prefer to push or drag them.

translation.text: 许多孩子蹬着三轮车，但大多数更喜欢推着或拖着车前进。

segments.text: Some children can cover the whole length of the pool without coming up for breath even once.

translation.text: 有些孩子一次也不用浮上来换气，就能游完整个泳池。

segments.text: Whether they will ever become future Olympic champions, only time will tell.

translation.text: 他们将来是否会成为奥运冠军，只有时间能够证明。

segments.text: Meanwhile, they should encourage those among us who cannot swim five yards before they are gasping for air.

translation.text: 与此同时，他们应该能鼓舞我们当中那些游不到五码就喘不过气来的人。
